title: The Cheap Dopamine Epidemic Stop Ruining Your Life
author: Dan Koe

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: The Cheap Dopamine Epidemic: Stop Ruining Your Life - YouTube (00:00) social media is the fast food of socialization porn is the fast food of intimacy video games are the fast food of achievement Netflix is the fast food of entertainment fast food itself is a combination of fat sugar and salt ingredients that used to be incredibly scarce that are combined and tested in infinite different ways to see which keeps consumers coming back for more in order to understand this cheap dopamine epidemic that we're going through we need to have a brief history lesson one way that societies can be categorized is (00:33) by their technoeconomic base now a technoeconomic base is in a nutshell their basic means of production so think the horsedrawn plow versus machine farming the things that we use to manage our basic needs and there are five of these technoeconomic bases the first is foraging which is hunting and Gathering so men hunted and women gathered the second is Horticultural which is just simple planting where women produce most of the food and the society's deities became female the third is agrarian which had animal drawn plows where women (01:09) couldn't continue farming due to an increase in miscarriages so their roles changed and the society's deities became men 80% of agrarian societies relied on male slave labor the thing to understand with these is that whoever produced the most Foods or whoever produced the most in general whether it was male or female became the deities of that Society and so the agrarian society marks a massive shift in culture for that reason this is the rise of the patriarchy so to say but the reason this patriarchy is a thing is (01:43) because of this shift in culture where women were the deities for most of human civilization so once this started happening in agrarian societies where women couldn't operate the horron plow because of too many miscarriages it was a darwinian disadvantage for women to do that thing so the roles shifted again and the men started to produce more of the food and this caused women to shift and take over more quote unquote traditional roles of tending to the house and the family and now because of the plow there was an abundance of food (02:17) so the men didn't have to work as much so they had more time for thinking and contemplation and this is what led to the invention of writing and Mathematics it also led to the ascending philosophies which we can talk about in a different video but that's when the spiritual side started to focus solely on the ascending and started to disconnect from the descending or the physios spere so this is when the demonization of things like money and sex became a thing for religions and spiritual people because they lost that (02:50) connection to the great chain of being physios spere biosphere new sphere they were focused solely on ascending and so people still hold this mindset that material Pursuits are all bad when they are not and they are actually connected to Spirit now the last two bases after The Agrarian were the industrial and informational take note here that the homogeneous species emerged around 2 million years ago and the homo sapiens species us emerged around 300,000 years ago and since the Industrial Age started about 300 years ago up until now that's (03:27) just a tiny fraction of what we are wired for so this Industrial Age and the informational age now changes everything because we're not wired for that thing human life spent most of its time in these foraging Horticultural and agrarian societies where none of what we had now exists and that is very important to this whole dopamine thing as the Industrial Age progressed fat sugar and salt became less and less scarce we could trade for salt in coastal areas and produce fat and sugar at a rapid rate between 1920 and 195 50 (04:00) things continued to change assembly lines and 9 to-5 jobs became a thing thanks Henry Ford for that and how humans work began to shift into the workforce we know today in short humans had more money and potential so with more resources with more trade during the Industrial Age humans became wealthier all around after 1950 fast food chain started popping up then the information age came online the Golden Age of Television allowed for more spread of information then a bit later the internet information began to grow (04:32) and spread like wildfire continuing to fast forward and we have Google Facebook and other soon to be massive companies competing in a race to the top now these weren't all bad at first but the desire for power caused technology to evolve in a way towards poor incentives towards power because everything was so new everything was happening so fast it had never been done before everyone was in that race to the top in other words everyone was trying to win the game now back to the topic of fat sugar and salt because we have to talk about digital (05:06) fat sugar and salt those resources fat sugar and salt humans aren't adapted to have those things in excess we're adapted for survival and so scarce ingredients like fat sugar and salt spurt dopamine into our brain because we need to remember them for survival we need to see them as important so once companies discovered this mechanism they can now test these different things fat sugar and salt in infinite different ways to see which kept the customers hooked and coming back for more and I mean just think of how many fast food (05:37) restaurants with quadruple patty burgers and quesa chalupa burita tacos like all the combinations of those things that continue coming out with new products and promoting so it keeps you going back to Taco Bell McDonald's Carl Jr Etc that's what we're talking about here but the thing is is that this is also reflected in the digital world at a much faster rate because it's digital it's not physical it can happen a lot faster so when these internet companies decided to adopt an advertising model for their growth and (06:11) their monetization they quickly realized that inflammatory headlines negativity and things like that led to capturing and retaining the most attention so now all they had to do was serve more users what they thought the users liked or they paid the most attention to and in comes the four view page the thing that you're stuck scrolling all day so in the digital world we can think of fat as fearmongering sugar as quick and desirable promises and salt as absolutistic takes in many cases not all cases the internet companies test (06:44) combinations of these and the algorithm feeds us as much as we want until we're mentally obese fear induced content spurts dopamine into our brains and narrows our thinking because we need the information for later survival now here we are quick money quick sex Quick Knowledge quick food quick entertainment a ticking time bomb that feels good at first but feels terrible later but there's more to the story dopamine isn't all bad we're living through a second Renaissance after all the Renaissance came after the Dark Ages by the way the (07:15) internet and social media for conscious and intentional individuals seeking truth and growth is the key to doing work you love and taking back control of your life now we need to to talk about how dopamine works because this is an extremely popular topic across social media but I don't think many of the videos do it justice so we're going to start with a quote from Daniel Z liberman who is the author of the molecule of more which is a book about dopamine in a broad sense saying something is important is another way of (07:50) saying it's linked to dopamine why because among the many things it does dopamine is an early warning system for the appearance of anything that can help us survive the thing here is is that most people don't understand dopamine and because they don't understand dopamine it tends to control their behavior it tends to control their life they can't become aware of the thought processes that are happening when that cheap dopamine is spurted into their brain and they can't work to reverse that problem and they don't know how to (08:17) reverse that problem because they can't see any other way dopamine isn't the pleasure molecule dopamine is the molecule of more but dopamine isn't the only neurotransmitter that provides feedback based on our behavior and perception there are two types of neurotransmitters the first are the down chemicals or the here and Nows so when you look down what do you see you see everything in your Parry personal space things that you can grab possess and control things that are within your possession the Here and Now chemicals (08:47) associated with what's in your immediate space allow you to savor enjoy or fight and run away from what you are experiencing so these chemicals are things like oxytocin neurop nephrine adrenaline serotonin endorphins and endocam endocannabinoids and then the second type of chemical is the up chemical or just dopamine so you can look outside you can look to the sky you can look in the windows while you're shopping you can look at the advertisements on TV you can look at this constant feed called social media and you can even look at (09:21) your memories of a past vacation that make you desire it now what do all of these things have in common they are things that you don't have things that are in your extra personal space dopamine motivates you to pursue to control and to possess what you don't have but want because it could be useful for your survival and the thing here is that it may not be useful for your survival now but it was in the past because in the past it was wise to gather more resources it was wise to have more sex and therefore more (09:51) offspring because you're trying to push your bloodline as far into the future as you can or else you go extinct as a species and other things like getting a horse and a sword because it allows you to fight battles and win and again progress your bloodline and survive so what's the issue and what do we do the issue is that we are so dopamine overloaded by the abundance of information forged by game theoretic principles that we've lost touch with reality relationships suffer because you possess a partner thanks to a dopamine (10:22) field motivation but you fail to reprogram your habits to appreciate and Savor that relationship after the honeymoon phase and we can apply this honeymoon phase to almost any area of life and we need to learn how to deal with that like when you're motivated to go to the gym you kind of get in this honeymoon phase with the gym you're focused you're making a lot of progress It's called newb begin gains you make a lot of progress really quickly and once that Fades out a lot of people quit because they get demotivated because (10:49) they're seeing slower progress so this is the shift from superficial Pursuits to a philosophical sense of Mastery when you're getting into a relationship and after that honeymoon phase or you're going to the gym or you're starting a new hobby or habit and you see that quick progression and you're just into this new interest eventually you have to appreciate it you have to master your craft you need to focus on the boring fundamentals and show up to them every single day and you need to move from dopamine to those down chemicals that (11:22) allow you to savor and appreciate what you have and if you don't know how to appreciate and Savor what you have watch the last video called why your 20s feel so meaningless where I talk about how to find more meaning in your life but for now as you're watching this video you just need to understand that dopamine is the anticipation molecule so if we want to enjoy the things we have we must train our minds away from the future oriented and move back to a sustained state of present oriented in other words be more soak in the present moment adopt (11:54) a mindfulness habit go on a walk stare at a leaf and find joy in the translucent Web of Life encapsulated in such a small green object that's the main habit you're probably missing now dopamine detoxes help for this reason you kind of remove all external more you you remove yourself from even potentially Desiring anything and you get stuck in this state of boredom so you're forced to find the appreciation in the boredom but you don't have to do a dopamine detox you simply have to change how you approach use and leverage (12:26) the technology that is destroying most people's lives here's the thing dopamine isn't a bad chemical it is the driving force behind success and failure the difference is long-term versus short-term gratification and that sentence isn't to be taken lightly because that difference is massive short-term gratification or cheap dopamine leads to entropy long-term gratification or earned dopamine leads to centropy entropy for what we're concerned with here is the gradual decline into chaos uncertainty and Disorder so you can think anxiety (13:04) overwhelm and that inescapable bubble of negative thoughts that seems to only get worse and worse because we can't make sense of the world anymore usually due to dwelling on the surface for too long now centropy or reversing entropy is the upward battle we are all facing as humans it is maintaining creating and improving order and certainty so you can think how challenge leads to fulfillment flow and a sense of purpose or control over your life now since social media is such a large and important part of our (13:32) lives and often necessary at this point that's what I want to focus on while social media can turn toxic fast it's difficult to ignore that it's an integral part of how we do and will work how we communicate and how we stay on the same page as everyone else so we can improve as a society now of course that last part is heavily being taken advantage of nobody seems to be on the same page if they just let the algorithm show them any and all information and they just shut off and they go to that wal scene where they have like that (14:02) helmet on where they're drinking soda from it if that's even no they're laying in the like capsule thing just watching TV all day and they're being drip fed this like pleasurable stuff and big drinks and whatever it's very dystopian if you just let social media control your life and you don't curate if you're not intentional about the information that you're consuming your life's going to be freaking terrible because the thing with that in the past the the unconscious Boomers when you talk to them you're just talking to a TV now gen (14:32) Z and Millennials when you talk to them the unconscious ones you're just talking to some polarizing social media feed when you talk to them if you can predict exactly what they're going to say next and they aren't willing to budge on their beliefs you're talking to a social media feed now that poses a very big problem because if you are conditioned by this social media feed and the algorithm continues to show you this short-term gratifying content that that keeps you in a state of stress and survival you can see the problem with (15:02) that the combination of fear and desire pull you away from the present moment and entropy increases what you thought was a harmless post was actually a combination of 50 short posts you read in unison that silently induces chaos in your mind you regress you stay the same you lock yourself into a paradigm of political ideologies and static opinions that aren't conducive to achieving anything worthwhile in your life now if the incentives of social media companies were different we would all be in a much better place but we are too deep into (15:33) the whole now and it's wise not to trust anyone especially a mega Corporation with increasing your quality of life that's on you now really quickly I want to talk about creating new digital environments for your success because as we talked about earlier uh digital information can spread and is created extremely rapidly and if this isn't conscious by you if it's not intentional if it's not curated then your life is moving in a negative Direction by definition but we can take advantage of that because since we have access to all (16:04) this information we can be intentional with what type of content that we consume that leads to more centropy or more order or more certainty or more success in our lives I want to start with this have you ever heard of the broken windows Theory it's pretty fascinating in a nutshell there was an experiment done where they study two different environments one with intentionally broken windows and one that looked clean and tidy in the area where they installed new windows and clean the area up crime decreased by (16:34) about 50% in the area where they broke windows and created a mess crime increased so why does this happen because your environment heavily conditions your behavior on the internet we're constantly exposing our minds to an area with thousands of broken windows a day digital environments condition your mind much faster than physical ones because there is more information available now here's the kicker few people realize that you chose to put yourself in that environment and you as an intentional individual can create a (17:06) new one your environment conditions who you are but most people forget that you create your environment social media is one of the greatest Technologies available to us and I don't mean to brag even though I kind of do that's why I'm saying it but social media has rarely been toxic or inflammatory to me it's like when I see something toxic inflammatory I I just scroll past it I'm sure some of that sticks in my mind and influences my behavior in one way or another but I would say that 95% of the time social media has been the engine (17:41) it's been the fuel for any success that I've had in life because you can learn anything you can build anything you don't need permission anymore you don't need credentials anymore you don't need to be an expert you don't need to be a degree or have a degree you simply need to have something valuable to share you need to have a mindset set that wants to collect valuable skill and insight from social media and therefore you have as many good and valuable inputs into your head as you want just through searching (18:13) and curating and then you produce valuable outputs so you can contribute back to it and hopefully turn social media into something better by your own personal efforts and you help other people in the meantime and as you can see as if you've watched any of my other videos if you want to turn that into some kind of income if you want to achieve the ultimate goal of being paid to be yourself or if you want to follow the alen Watts quote of sensible people get paid for doing what they enjoy doing it's more than possible now because just (18:45) think about it social media allows you to learn almost anything challenge your understanding and seek New Perspectives to get closer to truth it allows you to build a business or independent source of income you have indirect access to 5.5 billion people you can help in exchange for another form of value like money or attention that can be used to lead more people down a path of long-term gratification creating a ripple effect now if you want to understand all of this stuff in simple terms you can get the oneperson business (19:14) Launchpad via the link in the description it's 50% off through Black Friday weekend and this course finally answers the question that I've been asked so many different times is like okay I get the onep person business concept but how do I actually start so this is like a a beginner level very foundational what kind of software do you start an LLC what about taxes what skills do I learn what interest do I use what kind of product or service do I sell how do I sell it etc etc just all of that stuff laid out for you in plain (19:43) simple terms so check that out in the description if you're interested and the last thing social media allows you to do is to find potential employers investors team members or connections so creating high value content does the heavy lifting for you now the thing there and I'm building CeX at the moment my software startup and having an audience obviously makes it a lot easier you have potential customers already if I wanted to find investors for it which we not doing I could pretty easily I would think it's a lot easier to hire people (20:13) because you have an audience and referrals of great people that can come and work for you so starting any kind of business I believe should come after starting a oneperson business and just learning all the skills that you need to learn especially when you don't have any money or cash flow yet and the other thing is that you don't need a large audience to do this yes my audience has grown over the 5 to six years that I've done this but it's been 5 to six years of me doing it full-time now the thing with those three things that I listed (20:38) off being able to learn almost anything and challenge your understanding with longer form content being able to build a business and acquire new skills to do so to distribute your value and then finding potential employers if you want to get a job or investors or team members all of those things are syntropic they take time and effort they reverse the entropy and chaos you are so used to by providing meaningful inputs and outputs they are earned dopamine they lead to Greater complexity and Order allowing you to spot more (21:07) opportunities solve more problems create more value and profit in a meaningful way so since the social media companies won't change their own incentives it's up to you to create your own your own order your own environment your own Catalyst for Success so here's how in three steps the first is to curate your digital environment because the first offender here is short form content it can be useful for quick insights but can quickly devolve into a rabbit hole of memes and meaninglessness the problem is that we no longer challenge our beliefs (21:38) and understanding with books and articles whose incentives aren't to become a New York Times bestseller or to create the most inflammatory engagement bait headline if their main intention is status and that's the main intention because everyone is chasing status in one way or another then the information they distribute will be tailored to that so what I'm saying there is if the main intention is status them acquiring status then the article or the book or whatever it may be is probably mostly useless if they don't have the intention (22:10) to help and increase order in the reader's mind or go more long form go more in depth create more understanding and challenge in the world then it's probably not worth reading most of your time spent on the internet should be dedicated to long form content that challenges expands and nurtures your perspective read long read about more difficult Concepts read things that don't confirm your beliefs just to make you feel good reading can easily be confused with mental masturbation when it is so much more than that I would (22:39) encourage you to go watch one of my more recent videos on reading I think the thumbnail has me with a black line over my eyes that says read more books or something of that nature I go over my book recommendations inside of that I would argue that most of your future success is dependent on this single habit read longer things in other words consume content that aids in your development and not the other way around unfollow accounts that you know aren't serving your growth find long form writing videos and podcasts that you (23:10) enjoy listening to follow those long form creators on social media because they write better short form content that helps fill in the knowledge gaps of their long form if they're only on short form social media like Tik Tok and that's all they do no newsletter no YouTube channel no podcast or they don't write longer form content like if they're on X or in stagram if they don't write like threads or carousels or things like that then a lot of their growth comes from being polarizing being inflammatory being kind of Engagement (23:39) bity which is not bad all around they're probably not trying to do it and they're probably teaching something a lot better than people that are just posting memes and other things that don't really serve you aside for comedic purposes but I think you get what I'm saying so the more that you curate your digital environment the more you promote centropy or order or certainty the more you reduce or yeah reduce entropy in your life the less you fall into this chaos anxiety and overwhelm now the second thing after curating your digital (24:10) environment is to focus on a meaningful project why because a project is ordered you need to order your mind your projects frame your attention when you're working on something and you're building something that's what you're focused on distractions are hard to penetrate if the project is actually meaningful that's how you get into the Flow State that's how you become obsessed projects are your building blocks for a better future and as Naval said go do something great and your network will instantly emerge the other (24:38) thing is that now that you have good inputs because you're cating your digital environment you need to use that for good outputs otherwise it just creates more entropy in your mind if you're not actually producing with the ideas and information that you gather or read or consume if you aren't balancing consumption with creation then you still get mentally op be you still gain mental fat because you're doing the nutrition you're eating the food but you're not exercising dopamine doesn't just come from consuming information because (25:08) information isn't limited to the things you read and hear your habits what you do conditions your mind in a positive or negative Direction you can either work on a project that acts as a puzzle piece to your ideal lifestyle or you can fill your boredom with memes and pleasure that lead to disorder and Chaos if you don't have a project to build chances are you are already working on a project someone else's one that was assigned to you by a teacher or employer you'll spend 8 hours building their dreams but won't spend 1 hour building your own if (25:37) you're always building someone else's project you are not in control of your mind because your mind is framed by the project that you're working on the thing that you were producing the goal that you trying to achieve your mind literally interprets the world based on the goal that it has in its mind if your only goal is to wake up and go to the job most of your time is spent trying to numb the pain from that thing you're not building towards anything more if you have a business that you're pouring effort into every single morning then (26:02) you experience a lot more Serendipity in your life because you notice new things that can be applied to your business to improve it it's the entrepreneur versus the employee mindset you don't have to be in an entrepreneur to have an entrepreneurial mindset you can still be an employee that has high agency that's the term that's the difference between the two is agency how often are you doing your own thing and working towards your own goals whether it's in the company or it's not are you a slave or are you free is the question that I'm (26:35) asking and I'm not saying physical slavery I'm saying mental with attention are you riveted to someone else's dream or are you building your own that leads us into the third point after curating your digital environment building a meaningful project the last thing is earning your control and Freedom the question now is what do you build and I'm assuming you're starting from scratch so you have a little money saved up you may have a few skills you have have the ambition but you don't know where to start with building your own (27:01) thing and the thing is is that we're not in the Industrial Age anymore we're in the information age getting a job and investing your savings isn't the only option in fact if you couldn't tell people are losing trust in the conventional past promises to secure their future it's one of the worst options because your time energy and emotional well-being are at the control of a boss and your beliefs shape your reality and the opportunities available to you and if you haven't seen or just don't believe that teenagers of all (27:30) people are getting on Tik Tok or getting on social media they're building million-- dooll apps million-dollar products million-- dooll Services all of these things that they're just able to do because they took the time to study and they practice and they iterated and the whole argument of oh well uh not everyone can build a social media audience that's just plain wrong most people who've been consistent on social media in any meaningful amount have gone semi viral at least once they've had 10 ,000 to 50,000 to 100,000 people view (28:01) one post and if you're simply consistent and can try to replicate that it doesn't matter how large your audience is you're still getting in front of a ton of people that can see your products or services and you don't need that much traffic to make the glorified $10,000 a month especially as a oneperson business $50,000 $100,000 a month isn't a lot for an actual business now as a one-person business where you're still able to do this now thanks to technology social media and all of these things pop popping up that's a lot of money for one (28:30) single person so in business case anyone would scoff at 10,000 well not really but many people would scoff at $10,000 a month as a business but as one person that's quite good and it's more than doable all New Wealth is being generated in the digital space the idea space the Creator economy a place where people aren't limited by their connections or physical location because they can reach anyone with an internet connection and if you have the right skills like writing marketing and building which I teach in one person business Launchpad (29:03) and 2hour writer links in the description all you have to do is get what you build in front of a micr fraction of people that perceive what you built as valuable so create the content you want to see in the world build the product you want to see in the world create the content that stretches your mind and encourages you to grow as a writer attract like-minded people to your interest by building an audience these are the people you can help the most build the product that contributes to the development and self (29:28) actualization of others promote your product to your audience because you don't have a boss to do it for you anymore and earn your freedom that's it for this video thank you for watching I hope it was beneficial I had a lot of fun creating it like subscribe check out the
